questions: Write a program that prompts the user for their name and age using input(), then prints "Hello <name>, you are <age> years old."
Write a program that asks the user to enter three numbers, one per line, and then prints each number on a separate line prefixed with its position (e.g., "Number 1: 5").
Write a program that reads a line of text from the user and prints the same line in uppercase.
Write a program that prompts the user for their name and age, then prints a greeting that includes both values.
Write a program that reads a line of text from the user and prints the number of characters entered.
Write a program that continuously reads numbers until the user enters 0, then prints how many numbers were entered (excluding the terminating 0).
Write a program that reads two integers from the user and prints their sum, difference, product, and integer division result.
Write a program that calculates and prints the result of (a + b) * c given three integer variables a, b, and c.
Write a program that swaps the values of two variables using only arithmetic operators and prints the swapped values.
Write a program that reads two integers and prints their sum, difference, product, integer division result, and remainder using the appropriate arithmetic operators.
Write a program that reads a number and prints the result of raising it to the power of 3 using the exponentiation operator.
Write a program that reads three numbers and prints the largest of the three using comparison operators.
Write a program that reads an integer from the user and prints "Even" if the number is divisible by 2, otherwise prints "Odd".
Write a program that asks for a user's score (0-100) and prints "Pass" if the score is 40 or higher, otherwise prints "Fail".
Write a program that reads a string representing a day of the week and prints "Weekend" if it is Saturday or Sunday, otherwise prints "Weekday".
Write a program that asks for a student's income and prints "Eligible for scholarship" if the income is greater than 7000, otherwise prints "Not eligible for scholarship".
Write a program that asks for a number and prints "Divisible by 3" if the number is divisible by 3, otherwise prints "Not divisible by 3".
Write a program that asks for a number and prints "Divisible by both 3 and 5" if the number is divisible by both 3 and 5, otherwise prints "Not divisible by both".
Write a program that declares two integer variables a and b, assigns them the values 10 and 20, and prints their values separated by a space.
Write a program that stores your name in a string variable and your age in an integer variable, then prints a sentence like "My name is <name> and I am <age> years old."
Write a program that creates three variables x, y, z, assigns them any numeric values, and prints the result of x + y + z.
Write a program that creates two variables a and b, assigns them the values 10 and 20, computes their sum, and prints the result.
Write a program that defines a variable price with the value 50000 and prints a message showing the price formatted as currency.
Write a program that stores your name in a variable and prints a greeting that includes the name.
Write a program that reads two numbers as strings using input(), converts them to integers, and prints their sum.
Write a program that asks the user for their birth year as a string, converts it to an integer, calculates their age assuming the current year is 2026, and prints the age.
Write a program that reads a floating‑point number as a string, converts it to a float, multiplies it by 2, and prints the result.
Write a program that reads a score as a string, converts it to an integer, and then uses it in an if‑else statement to categorize the student.
Write a program that prompts the user for two numbers as strings, converts them to floats, and prints their division result.
Write a program that reads a user's age as input, converts it to an integer, and prints a message based on whether the age is at least 18.
Write a program that iterates over a list of fruit names ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry'] and prints each fruit on a separate line using a for loop.
Write a program that uses a for loop with range(5) to print the numbers 0 through 4.
Write a program that asks the user to enter a word and then uses a for loop to print each character of the word on a new line.
Write a program that iterates over the numbers from 1 to 10 and prints only the even numbers using a for loop.
Write a program that prints numbers from 1 to 10 using a for loop.
Write a program that asks the user for two integers A and B, then prints all numbers between A and B (inclusive) using a for loop.
Write a program that prints a multiplication table for numbers 1 to 3 using nested for loops.
Write a program that prints weeks and days: for week numbers 1 to 2, print "Week X - Day Y" for day numbers 1 to 3.
Write a program that generates a right‑triangle pattern of asterisks with 4 rows using nested loops.
Write a program that iterates over the lists ["red", "green"] and ["apple", "pear"] and prints every color‑fruit combination.
Write a program that creates and prints a 3×3 grid of sequential numbers using nested loops.
Write a program that prints numbers from 1 to 5 using a while loop.
Write a program that repeatedly asks the user to enter a number until they enter 0, then prints the total sum of all entered numbers.
Write a program that counts down from 10 to 1 using a while loop and prints each number.
Write a program that computes the factorial of a given positive integer using a while loop.
Write a program that keeps prompting the user for a password until the correct password "secret" is entered, then prints "Access granted".
Write a program that prints the numbers from 1 to 5 using a while loop.
Write a program that creates a list of integers, inserts the value 11 at index 0, and then prints the updated list.
Write a program that starts with a list [1, 2, 3], extends it with another list [4, 5, 6], and prints the final list.
Write a program that removes the element at index 2 from a list using pop and prints the removed element and the remaining list.
Write a program that demonstrates how duplicate values are allowed in a list by adding repeated elements and printing the list.
Write a program that creates a set with the values {1, 2, 3}, adds the number 4 using the add method, and prints the resulting set.
Write a program that attempts to add duplicate values to a set and shows that the set contains each element only once.
Write a program that removes an element from a set using the discard method and prints the set before and after removal.
Write a program that creates a dictionary with a key "name" and value "Alice", then updates the value to "Bob" and prints the dictionary.
Write a program that adds a new key-value pair to a dictionary and prints all keys and values.
Write a program that demonstrates that duplicate keys are overwritten by inserting the same key with a different value and printing the final dictionary.
Write a function called factorial that takes an integer n and returns its factorial using a while loop.
Write a function that receives a list and returns a new list with all duplicate elements removed.
Write a function that takes a dictionary and a key, and returns the corresponding value if the key exists or returns "Not found" otherwise.
Write a program that defines a function called greet that prints "Hello, World!" and then calls the function.
Write a program that defines a function sum_two that takes two numbers, returns their sum, and prints the result for the numbers 7 and 5.
Write a program that defines a function show_year that prints the current year using the datetime module and then calls the function.
Write a function average_three that takes three numbers as arguments and returns their average.
Write a function list_length that takes a list as an argument and returns its length.
Write a function count_char that takes a string and a single character, and returns how many times the character appears in the string.
Write a function max_of_two that takes two integers and returns the larger one.
Write a function that takes two numbers as arguments and returns their sum.
Write a function that accepts a list of numbers and returns the count of elements in the list.
Write a program that defines a class Laptop with attributes processor, ram, and price. Create an instance of Laptop, set its attributes to "i5", 16, and 75000, and then print all attribute values.
Write a program that defines a class Laptop with a method display_specs that prints the processor, ram, and price of the laptop. Create an object and call the method.
Write a program that defines a class Laptop, creates two Laptop objects with different specifications, and stores them in a list. Then iterate over the list and print each laptop's specifications.
Write a program that defines a class Student with attributes name and register_number and a method display that prints both attributes.
Write a program that defines a class Fruit with a color attribute set in the constructor, then creates two objects Apple and Banana with different colors and prints their colors.
Write a program that defines a class Laptop with attributes ram and processor and a method specs that returns a formatted string describing the laptop.

Write a program that defines a class Laptop with an __init__ method that initializes processor, ram, and price attributes from parameters. Create an instance using the constructor and print its attributes.
Write a program that defines a class Laptop with an __init__ method that sets a default price of 0 if no price is provided. Create one laptop with a price and one without, then print both prices.

Write a program that defines a class Laptop whose __init__ method takes a single string "specs" in the format "processor-ram-price" (e.g., "i7-32-120000") and parses it to set the corresponding attributes. Create an object using this format and display its attributes.
Write a program that defines a class Car whose __init__ method takes make, model, and year as parameters and stores them as attributes.
Write a program that defines a class Person with an __init__ method that takes name and age, stores them, and includes a method greet that prints a greeting using the name.
Write a program that defines a class Rectangle with an __init__ method that takes width and height, stores them, and provides a method perimeter that returns the rectangle's perimeter.

Write a program that defines a base class Animal with a method sound that prints "Generic animal sound", defines a subclass Dog that overrides sound to print "Woof!", creates instances of both classes, and calls their sound methods.

Write a program that creates a class hierarchy Vehicle -> Car -> ElectricCar, where each subclass adds an attribute (e.g., number_of_wheels, battery_capacity) and inherits a method describe that prints all attributes, then demonstrates creating an ElectricCar and calling describe.

Write a program that defines two classes Flyable and Swimmable each with a method move that prints a specific action, then defines a class Duck that inherits from both and overrides move to print "Duck can both fly and swim", and finally creates a Duck instance and calls its move method.

Write a program that defines a base class Person with an attribute name and a method introduce that prints "I am <name>". Define a subclass Student that inherits Person, adds an attribute grade via its constructor, and overrides introduce to also print "My grade is <grade>". Create a Student and call introduce.

Write a program that defines a base class Vehicle with a method start that prints "Vehicle started". Define a subclass Car that inherits Vehicle and overrides start to print "Car started". Instantiate both classes and call their start methods.

Write a program that defines a base class Shape with a method area that returns 0. Define a subclass Rectangle that inherits Shape, has length and width attributes, and overrides area to return length * width. Create a Rectangle object and print its area.

Write a program that defines two classes, Cat and Dog, each with a method sound that prints "Meow" and "Bark" respectively. Write a function make_sound that takes an animal object and calls its sound method. Demonstrate polymorphism by passing instances of both classes to make_sound.
Write a program that prompts the user for two integers, adds them, and uses try/except to handle ValueError if the input is not a valid integer.
Write a program that divides a number by user‑provided divisor and catches ZeroDivisionError, printing an appropriate message.
Write a program that reads a number from input, converts it to int inside a try block, and prints "Valid number" if no exception occurs, otherwise prints "Invalid input".
Write a program that uses a try/except block to open a file named "data.txt" for reading and prints an error message if the file does not exist.
Write a program that creates a new text file called "fruits.txt", writes the words "Apple" and "Orange" on separate lines, closes the file, then reopens it and prints its contents.

Write a program that reads a file named "data.txt" and prints the number of lines it contains. If the file does not exist, the program should create it with a single line saying "Empty file" and then print the line count.
Write a program that appends the user-provided fruit name to the end of "fruits.txt" without overwriting the existing content, then displays the updated file contents.
Write a program that attempts to read from a file that has been closed, catches the resulting exception, and prints a friendly error message indicating that the file is not open.
